title: skopeo copy --preserve-digests: no descriptor found for reference
body:
I'm having an issue using the "oci" transport while preserving digests.  I am initially able to copy from "docker" to "oci", but I am unable to reference the "oci" image by name afterwards.  Here's an example:

Copy from Docker registry to OCI compliant directory "images":
```
# skopeo copy --preserve-digests "docker://rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20" "oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20"
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 096f4a190c22 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5843afab3874 skipped: already exists
Copying config 933989e117 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

Inspect image:
```
# skopeo inspect "oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20"
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20": no descriptor found for reference "rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20"
```

Copy image to local Docker registry (localhost:5000):
```
# skopeo copy oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20 "docker://localhost:5000/rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20" --dest-tls-verify=false
FATA[0000] initializing source oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20: no descriptor found for reference "rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20"
```

If I do not use `--preserve-digests`, everything works as expected: 
```
# skopeo copy "docker://rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20" "oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20"
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 096f4a190c22 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5843afab3874 skipped: already exists
Copying config 71d619a73e done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# skopeo copy oci:images:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20 "docker://localhost:5000/rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20" --dest-tls-verify=false
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5843afab3874 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 096f4a190c22 skipped: already exists
Copying config 71d619a73e done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

Skopeo version:
```
# skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.12.0 commit: a55290973794d93f602a027e795bf510bd3cad01
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

There isn’t enough evidence in the above (see `skopeo --debug copy`, search for `Content-Type from manifest GET is`), but I guess that the original image is using a Docker manifest schema, not an OCI schema.

`--preserve-digests`, necessarily, disables image format conversion, so there is no way to produce a correct OCI repository in that case; this sequence of commands must fail _somehow_.

Still, the way it does fail is admittedly quite confusing.
- One option would be for writes to `oci` to explicitly check, and reject, writes of non-OCI manifests. That’s plausible, but not quite consistent with how we do allow `skopeo copy --format` to override destination’s lists of supported formats at least in _some_ cases.
- The reads from OCI currently completely silently skip non-OCI manifests, causing this confusing `no descriptor found` error. That seems like a clear local improvement we can make.

With https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1922 :

```
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "oci:images2:rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20": reference "rancher/local-path-provisioner:v0.0.20" matches unsupported manifest MIME types ["application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"] 
```
Makes sense.  Thanks for the quick update!
